nurseryCosts.note: Coram Family and Childcare annual survey. Full-time = 50 hours/week, 50 weeks/year average.

workforce.note: Many childcare workers paid below NLW in 2023 — gap closed with NLW rise in April 2024.

fundingGap.note: NDNA and Coram estimate gap between government hourly rate and actual delivery cost for 3-4-year-old free hours.

sources.name: DfE
sources.dataset: Education provision: children under 5 years of age

sources.name: OECD
sources.dataset: Family database: childcare and early education

metadata.methodology: Nursery costs from Coram Family and Childcare annual survey of nursery providers in England. Full-time defined as 50 hours/week, 50 weeks/year. Funding gap analysis by NDNA (National Day Nurseries Association) and Coram comparing DfE hourly rate with actual provision costs. OECD comparisons based on public spending on childcare as % of GDP.
knownIssues: Free hours expansion from 2024 onward significantly changes the landscape — older cost data not directly comparable
Nursery costs vary enormously by region — national averages mask London premium
Provider count includes all Ofsted-registered childcare, not just nurseries — includes childminders
